summary: The branch implements the requested Node 22 pin, removes the CLI env-file flag, imports the env loader first, and adds loadEnv tests. I found one issue in the new env loader: it suppresses more failures than the spec calls for. I could not verify the full npm gate in this read-only sandbox because Vitest/server tests need filesystem/socket writes and failed with EPERM.

findings.title: Env loader suppresses non-missing .env failures
findings.claim: The new catch block treats every `process.loadEnvFile` exception as a harmless missing `.env`. The spec only calls for no-op behavior when the file is missing or the API is unavailable; with this implementation, an unreadable `.env`, bad path, or other load failure silently boots with expected local env vars absent, making configuration problems look like ordinary unconfigured OAuth state.
findings.suggestion: Return false when `process.loadEnvFile` is unavailable, catch and ignore only the missing-file error, and rethrow other load failures.
